sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Jerry, a 45-year-old male, presented with an ankle injury sustained while playing basketball with his 10-year-old son.
He landed awkwardly after being hit by his son, causing a twisting motion that resulted in pain on the outside of his ankle.
The ankle is swollen and bruised, with exquisite tenderness on the lateral and medial deltoid regions.
Jerry reports difficulty walking and feeling instability in his ankle, which has hindered his ability to continue working out and participating in basketball.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The examination revealed swelling on the lateral side of the ankle, exquisite tenderness on the lateral and medial deltoid regions, and brisk capillary refill.
The skin is intact, and there is no evidence of malalignment.
Test Results:
X-ray results of the right ankle showed a displaced lateral malleolus fracture at the Weber C level, with no evidence of medial or posterior malleolar fractures.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a lateral malleolar fracture, which is unstable and requires surgical intervention.
Problem list: Lateral malleolar fracture; active
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 7.5 mg PO QID for pain and inflammation.
I advised Jerry to ice the ankle and keep it elevated to reduce swelling.
I also recommended that he use crutches to avoid putting weight on the ankle.
I encouraged him to continue coaching his son's basketball team and reassured him that he will be able to return to exercise within three months.
I will follow up with him in a few weeks to assess his progress.
Follow-up: I will see Jerry in three months to evaluate the healing of the fracture and provide further guidance on his rehabilitation.
